performance_overview: Through 27 trading sessions (inception 2026-06-01 through 2026-07-02 EOD) Bull has completed 7 trades: 3W/4L (43% win rate, avg +0.15%/trade). Cumulative: Bull -0.0612% vs SPY unavailable (persistent TLS error). Equity flat at $99,938.80. System transitioned to MA-20/60 trend scan for evening agent; market-open agent continues intraday RSI/momentum strategy. Zero overnight holds ever.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket; enforce hard floor+ceiling (RSI between 55 and 60 inclusive); n=2 (directionally clean but thin sample — maintain until n≥10)
avoid: RSI 60-65 at entry: 0/3 wins (0%), avg -1.06% — every overbought chase entry has lost; hard ceiling at 60, no exceptions ever
RSI 65-75 at entry: 0/1 wins (0%), avg -0.61% — confirmed gap-up trap territory; skip on sight

Gap-up opens with RSI >60 on opening bar: zero qualifications across all 26 sessions; any name whose live RSI exceeds 60 on the first 1-min bar is a hard skip regardless of setup quality, conviction, or premarket RSI

Opening-bar RSI surge pattern: premarket-clean RSI names (PANW 57, AMGN 58 premarket Jun 29; D 58.2 premarket Jun 30; AMGN 54.4 premarket Jul 1) have routinely surged past 60 on the opening bar — the gap-up trap is systematic; do NOT enter within first 5-10 minutes of open even if premarket RSI was clean

Current defensives/utilities/managed-care universe (DTE, WEC, CNC, D, FRT, ED, EIX, NSC, KIM, AMGN, MTB, FITB, CL, SJM, CNC, ES): chronic ATR% compression 0.042-0.189% far below 0.30% floor; this entire universe is effectively dead for this strategy and MUST be replaced
overnight_hold_insights: Zero overnight carries across all 26 sessions. The six-criteria gate (profitable + trailing-stop-active + above EMA-21 + no earnings within 2 days + sentiment not negative + 5% gap-risk cushion) has never been simultaneously satisfied. Policy is sound and conservative — no change warranted. Persistent SPY DOWN regime increases overnight gap-down risk further; the gate is protective.

notes_for_next_session:
FOR MARKET-OPEN AGENT (next trading day — likely Monday July 7, 2026 — verify calendar):

CALENDAR ALERT: July 4, 2026 is a Saturday. NYSE likely closes Friday July 3 as the observed holiday. Confirm market schedule before proceeding. If July 3 is a trading day (half-day), treat cautiously — low volume.

STATUS: Zero open positions. Equity $99,938.80. No death cross exits pending.

=== EVENING SCAN RESULTS (July 2, 2026 — MA-20/60 Trend) ===
3 golden cross candidates after earnings filter (0 blackouts):

1. SCHW (Charles Schwab, Financials) — close=$96.96, ATR=2.32 (2.4%), vol=757k
   EMA20=$91.79, EMA60=$91.79 — EMA gap only $0.0025 (extremely shallow first-day cross)
   CONCERN: Highest ATR but near-zero EMA separation; elevated fake-out risk; price moved far above cross.

2. PCG (PG&E Corp, Utilities/regulated) — close=$17.05, ATR=0.38 (2.2%), vol=950k
   EMA20=$16.82, EMA60=$16.81 — EMA gap only $0.003 (equally shallow)
   CONCERN: Low-priced name, very shallow cross. Regulated utility sector.

3. XLU (Utilities Select Sector ETF) — close=$45.75, ATR=0.69 (1.5%), vol=1.55M
   EMA20=$45.09, EMA60=$45.06 — EMA gap $0.028 (widest of three, but still shallow)
   NOTE: Sector ETF = inherently lower volatility. Best liquidity of the three. Defensive positioning.

EXECUTION GUIDANCE:
- All three are valid entries for the MA-20/60 trend strategy (no earnings conflicts for 14 days)
- Size conservatively given shallow crosses and potential holiday-week low volume
- If holding through a long weekend (e.g. July 4 weekend), be aware of gap risk on Monday open
- Monitor for EMA20 re-crossing below EMA60 (death cross) — at this separation distance that can happen quickly
- XLU is the most defensive/liquid; SCHW offers highest ATR if momentum holds

BUG FIXED (July 2): trend_scan.py was double-prepending DATA_DIR in read_json/write_json calls, causing universe to return None and outputs to write to data/data/. Fixed to use absolute paths. Also fixed check_earnings.py path mismatch (was looking for watchlist.json, not watchlist_trend.json).
